channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hisense's new 4K laser TV delivers up to 150 inches of picture, and 6.2.2 channels of Dolby Atmos sound
item.description: Hisense has revealed its new laser TV ahead of CES 2025, and it's going big on both projection size and sound quality.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: If your Google Nest smart speaker has been giving you the silent treatment, there's now a fix
item.description: Some users found that their devices stopped responding to voice commands between Christmas and New Year's Day.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Easy internet sleuth tricks you’ll wish you knew sooner
item.description: Here are some powerful online sleuthing tricks you can use to check the credibility of a website, see old pictures of places you've lived and even learn more about your neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Indiana bill could force kids under 16 off of social media without parental permission
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS--- If State Sen. Mike Bohacek’s (R-Michiana Shores) bill passes next session, social media platforms would need to block kids under 16 from accessing their content if they don’t receive their parents’ permission to use their sites. ”All we’re trying to do is protect kids,” Bohacek said.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Adamson, NewsNation

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Graviton progress: 50% of new AWS instances run on Amazon custom silicon
item.description: Half of all instances spun up on Amazon Web Services over the past two years used Graviton, Amazon’s custom silicon built on Arm processor technology, rather than x86 processors.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Americans more likely to embrace driverless cars, but still skeptical: Survey
item.description: Americans are more likely to embrace driverless cars but are still skeptical of them, according to a new survey from YouGov. In the survey, 35 percent of Americans said when it comes to driverless cars’ advancement, they are “very enthusiastic” or “somewhat enthusiastic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Joel Kaplan to replace Nick Clegg as Meta global policy leader
item.description: Meta will switch up its global policy team in the new year and has appointed former George W. Bush administration official Joel Kaplan to replace Nick Clegg as the company's chief global affairs officer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Microsoft claims Edge had a great year, boasts soaring browser user numbers
item.description: Microsoft claims 10 billion GenAI chats happened from within Edge, but browser still lags behind Chrome.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: AI, power availability and Intel’s future
item.description: As 2025 kicks off, it’s time to dust off the crystal ball. Here are my data center predictions for the coming year. 1) Trump backs off on tech tariffs For all the handwringing, Trump has a history of not following through on his threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes
item.description: AI tools are accelerating the development of deepfake schemes and disinformation campaigns that led to fraud The IRS is trying to keep up. The post IRS deploys AI tools to combat emerging tech’s role in new fraud schemes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Hackers have posted some RIBridges data on the dark web, McKee says
item.description: The state disclosed earlier this month that cybercriminals had gained access to the state's IT system for social services.
item.dc:creator: Ted Nesi, NewsNation

item.title: Multitenant data management with TiDB
item.description: In a previous article, we explored various approaches to managing data for multitenant applications. We examined the benefits, compromises and limitations of different deployment models, including share-nothing, share-everything, and hybrid.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump weighs in on H-1B debate, backs immigration visas
item.description: “I’ve always liked the visas, I have always been in favor of the visas. That’s why we have them,” Trump said in a phone interview with The New York Post published Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation
